title: Adoption risks & next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion
summary: Onboarding completion improved after the docs refresh, but adoption is still blocked by setup/credential failures that lack a public recovery path. Support is repeatedly resolving issues using an internal checklist not reflected in docs, and pilot expansion is explicitly gated on making recovery and evidence/citations observable without human intervention.

claims.text: Quickstart completion increased from 44% to 58% after the copy refresh, with fewer exits at the first API call step.
The largest remaining drop-off occurs during local setup and credential configuration due to environment-specific errors; new users without a configured environment still hit the same setup wall.

Support tickets are dominated by environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials copied from the wrong workspace; users often need an internal checklist to recover.
Tickets often reopen because users restart the quickstart and reproduce the same failed state, indicating the product/docs do not provide a reliable recovery loop after partial setup.

Pilot expansion is available (two additional teams next month) but is blocked by the visible brittleness of setup recovery; expansion should wait until failed-install guidance is observable in the lab.

openQuestions: Which specific recovery steps from the internal support checklist should be productized vs documented, and what is the minimum public version that resolves most cases?
What instrumentation will confirm that users successfully recover from partial setup (not just complete the quickstart with a pre-existing token)?
What is the lab/UX mechanism to visibly show evidence provenance (analytics vs support vs interviews) and source citations during pilot demos?
recommendation: Prioritize a “setup recovery path” as the next product-quality improvement: publish and integrate a public recovery checklist/flow that resolves the top failure modes (stale CLI installs, missing env vars, workspace credential mismatches, repeated setup attempts) and can be demonstrated end-to-end without support intervention. This directly addresses the biggest onboarding drop-off and top support drivers, and it is the explicit gate for scheduling pilot expansion.
